question.text: What is the maximum gap between two meetings of the Audit Committee of an AMC?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** At least 4 meetings shall be called in a financial year. The time gap between meetings shall not be more than 120 days. **The Trap:** 90 days (quarterly) might seem logical but the regulation allows up to 120 days. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Quorum requires two members or one-third of members, whichever is greater, with at least two independent directors.
